meta.description: Critical + High priority fixes for Birkin agent platform. Each task maps to one branch and one PR. Subtasks are ordered by dependency.
meta.execution_style: Claude Code should complete tasks in `order` sequence. Each task has its own branch. After subtasks complete, run `verification` commands; if green, commit per `commit_message` and open PR.

tasks.title: Fix Anthropic model IDs
tasks.rationale: Hardcoded model strings (claude-opus-4-20250805 etc.) do not exist in Anthropic API. Any call with the default model returns 404. This blocks verification of every other task.

subtasks.detail: Replace _DEFAULT_MODEL with 'claude-sonnet-4-6' (cheaper than opus for default). Update _MODEL_CAPS dict keys to the three real model IDs: claude-opus-4-6, claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-haiku-4-5-20251001. Keep context_window=200000 for all three.

Find the Anthropic model dropdown options (search for 'claude-opus' or 'claude-sonnet'). Replace old model IDs with the three new ones. Add a friendly display name map: opus-4-6 → 'Claude Opus 4.6', sonnet-4-6 → 'Claude Sonnet 4.6', haiku-4-5 → 'Claude Haiku 4.5'.
Update any assertions that reference old model strings. Add a parameterized test that the three new model IDs resolve to a valid ModelCapabilities entry.

acceptance_criteria: _DEFAULT_MODEL is 'claude-sonnet-4-6'
_MODEL_CAPS contains exactly the three real model IDs
All tests pass
WebUI model dropdown shows the three new options
tasks.commit_message: fix: update Anthropic model IDs to claude-{opus,sonnet,haiku}-4.x
tasks.rollback_notes: Single-file revert. No data migration needed.

tasks.title: Wire SQLite close into FastAPI lifespan
tasks.rationale: Thread-local SQLite connections are never explicitly closed. WAL lock files can persist across restarts. Also foundation for #7 (executor shutdown) and #3 (auth init).

subtasks.detail: Add `close_all()` method that iterates all thread-local connections created by this store and closes them. Track connections in a WeakSet or module-level list keyed by thread ID. The existing close() only closes current thread's connection — keep it but add close_all() for shutdown.

Add module-level reset functions: reset_session_store(), reset_wiki_memory(), reset_telegram_adapter(), reset_dispatcher() — each sets the singleton back to None so garbage collection runs. reset_telegram_adapter already exists; align the rest.

Replace startup/shutdown event handlers (or add, if missing) with a FastAPI lifespan async context manager. On shutdown: call get_session_store().close_all(), stop telegram polling if running, reset all singletons. Wire lifespan into FastAPI app factory.

New test file. Test: create TestClient with FastAPI app, make a chat request, close client, verify SQLite WAL file is cleanly released (no .db-wal lingering or assert it can be reopened in exclusive mode).

verification: python -c "from birkin.gateway.app import create_app; app = create_app(); print('ok')"
Manual: start uvicorn, Ctrl+C, restart — no WAL lock errors
acceptance_criteria: FastAPI lifespan is registered on the app
SessionStore.close_all() exists and closes all thread connections
Server shutdown cleanly releases SQLite locks
All existing tests still pass
tasks.commit_message: fix: wire SQLite connections into FastAPI lifespan for clean shutdown
tasks.rollback_notes: Revert app.py changes; fall back to no-op shutdown.

tasks.title: Add Bearer token auth middleware
tasks.rationale: Any endpoint (shell tool, API keys, wiki edit) is unauthenticated. Required before Docker / 0.0.0.0 binding is safe.

subtasks.detail: Define `require_auth` FastAPI dependency: reads `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header OR `birkin_session` cookie; compares to env var BIRKIN_AUTH_TOKEN (via hmac.compare_digest). If token env is unset and host is 127.0.0.1, allow (dev mode). Raise HTTPException(401) on mismatch. Also expose `exempt_paths` list: /api/health, /static/*, /webhooks/telegram/*, /api/auth/bootstrap.

Add POST /api/auth/bootstrap: accepts {token: string}; if token matches BIRKIN_AUTH_TOKEN, set HttpOnly secure cookie `birkin_session=<token>` with SameSite=Lax; returns {ok: true}. Also GET /api/auth/status for UI to check if auth is configured.
Register a global middleware that checks require_auth for non-exempt paths. Alternative: use FastAPI Depends on router include. Prefer middleware to keep routes clean.

On `birkin` / `birkin serve` startup: if BIRKIN_AUTH_TOKEN is not set AND host != 127.0.0.1, print warning and refuse to start. If token not set and host is localhost, print informational notice about dev mode.
On first run, if .env has no BIRKIN_AUTH_TOKEN, generate one via `python -c 'import secrets; print(secrets.token_urlsafe(32))'` and append to .env. Print the token to stdout for the user.
Mirror start.sh behavior on Windows using Python to generate the token.
Add BIRKIN_AUTH_TOKEN= line with comment explaining auto-generation.

On first load, check /api/auth/status. If auth required, read token from URL query param `?token=...` (passed by start script) and call /api/auth/bootstrap to set cookie. Strip token from URL after. If cookie exists, proceed normally.

Test cases: (1) no token env + localhost → 200; (2) token env set + no header → 401; (3) token env set + correct header → 200; (4) token env set + correct cookie → 200; (5) exempt path /api/health → always 200; (6) bootstrap endpoint sets cookie correctly.

verification: BIRKIN_AUTH_TOKEN=test curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer test' http://127.0.0.1:8321/api/sessions # should be 200
acceptance_criteria: Requests without valid token are rejected (401) when BIRKIN_AUTH_TOKEN is set
Localhost dev mode (no token env) still works for first-time users
WebUI transparently authenticates via cookie
Telegram webhook endpoint remains accessible (its own auth)
tasks.commit_message: feat(security): add Bearer token auth middleware for API endpoints
tasks.rollback_notes: Remove auth.py and middleware registration. Existing unauthenticated behavior restored.

tasks.title: Harden Shell tool with allowlist
tasks.rationale: Current blocklist is trivially bypassable (bash -c, variable expansion, busybox prefix). Switch to explicit allowlist + shell metachar detection. Document as 'not a sandbox'.

subtasks.detail: Replace _BLOCKED_PATTERNS/_BLOCKED_PREFIXES logic with: (1) parse command via shlex.split; (2) check first token (command name) is in _ALLOWED_COMMANDS = {'ls','cat','grep','rg','find','git','head','tail','wc','pwd','echo','date','which','file','stat'}; (3) reject if command contains shell metachars: ; && || | > < ` $( ${ (scan raw string); (4) keep existing timeout and output truncation. Add env var BIRKIN_SHELL_ALLOWLIST to extend the set. Add BIRKIN_SHELL_SANDBOX=off to bypass (logs a WARNING). Keep the old block patterns as an additional defense-in-depth layer after allowlist check.

Add test cases: (1) 'ls -la' → allowed; (2) 'rm -rf /' → denied (not in allowlist); (3) 'bash -c "ls"' → denied (bash not in allowlist); (4) 'ls | grep foo' → denied (pipe metachar); (5) 'ls $(whoami)' → denied (subshell); (6) 'ls && echo done' → denied (chain); (7) BIRKIN_SHELL_ALLOWLIST=curl,ls → curl allowed; (8) BIRKIN_SHELL_SANDBOX=off → rm denied only by legacy blocklist.

Add new section '## Security model' near the end. State: (a) shell tool uses an allowlist, not a sandbox; (b) LLM-generated commands are still code execution — run only on trusted deployments; (c) link to #1-3 fix for future Phase 2 sandbox plans. Explicitly say 'Do not expose the API server to the public internet without BIRKIN_AUTH_TOKEN set'.

acceptance_criteria: Only allowlisted commands execute
Shell metacharacters trigger rejection with clear error
BIRKIN_SHELL_ALLOWLIST env var extends the set
README Security section present
New test cases all pass
tasks.commit_message: security(shell): replace blocklist with allowlist + metachar rejection
tasks.rollback_notes: Single file revert. Legacy blocklist still present as second layer.

tasks.title: Add Telegram secret_token header verification
tasks.rationale: Webhook URL leaks bot_token if logged. Telegram recommends X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token header for webhook authentication.

subtasks.detail: Modify set_webhook(): generate a random secret via secrets.token_urlsafe(32), pass as 'secret_token' to Telegram setWebhook API, return it in response dict. Add new method verify_secret(header_value: str, expected: str) using hmac.compare_digest.
Add 'telegram_webhook_secret' field to config schema. Save the secret returned from set_webhook here.

In POST /telegram/webhook (set_webhook endpoint): persist returned secret to config. In POST /webhooks/telegram/{bot_token}: read X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token header, compare to config['telegram_webhook_secret'] via hmac.compare_digest. If missing or mismatch AND secret is configured → 403. Keep bot_token path check as defense-in-depth.
Test cases: (1) webhook with correct secret header → 200; (2) wrong header → 403; (3) missing header when secret configured → 403; (4) no secret in config (legacy) → path bot_token check still works.

verification: Manual: re-register webhook via WebUI, check Telegram admin log
acceptance_criteria: Webhook registration stores a per-install secret
Incoming webhook requests without correct header are rejected
Legacy installs without secret still function via bot_token path check
tasks.commit_message: security(telegram): verify X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token on webhook receive
tasks.rollback_notes: Remove secret verification; bot_token path match restored as only check.

tasks.title: Split routes.py into modular routers
tasks.rationale: routes.py is a 1004-line god file. Split for maintainability and to unblock plugin system (Phase 2). This PR is pure file movement, no behavior change.

subtasks.detail: Export a single `all_routers` list containing each submodule's `router`. Makes app.py registration a one-liner.
Move /health endpoint. prefix='/api'.
Move /chat and /chat/stream endpoints (routes.py lines ~45-278). prefix='/api'. Move related helpers if any are chat-specific.
Move /settings, /settings/keys, /settings/providers endpoints + _ALLOWED_KEY_NAMES constant.
Move /sessions/* endpoints.
Move /wiki/* endpoints including upload.
Move /workflows/* endpoints.
Move /telegram/* endpoints (status, health, webhook mgmt, polling, send-test).
Move /webhooks/telegram/{bot_token} endpoint.
Replace `from birkin.gateway.routes import router` with `from birkin.gateway.routers import all_routers` and iterate include_router.
Remove after all content migrated.
Update imports if any tests import from birkin.gateway.routes. URL paths stay identical so HTTP-level tests need no change.

verification: Manual: hit each endpoint group via WebUI
acceptance_criteria: routes.py no longer exists
Each router file < 300 LOC
All tests pass without path changes
All URL paths unchanged
tasks.commit_message: refactor(gateway): split routes.py into modular routers
tasks.rollback_notes: Merge router files back into routes.py via git revert.

tasks.title: Reuse ThreadPoolExecutor in _run_async
tasks.rationale: Current sync→async bridge spawns a fresh thread+event-loop per tool call. Reuse an executor for lower latency and GC pressure.

subtasks.detail: Replace the `_run_async` helper that creates threading.Thread per call. Use a module-level ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=4, thread_name_prefix='birkin-tool'). Submit asyncio.run as a job, .result() to wait. Expose module-level `shutdown_executor()` for lifespan hook.
In lifespan shutdown (from T2), call shutdown_executor(wait=True).

Benchmark-style test: execute 10 tool calls via sync agent.chat, assert total time < threshold (loose threshold, goal is regression detection not performance claim). Verify executor is reused (thread names repeat).

verification: Manual: run agent with 10+ tool calls, check process thread count stays flat
acceptance_criteria: _run_async uses a shared executor
Executor is cleanly shut down on server stop
Tests pass
tasks.commit_message: perf(agent): reuse ThreadPoolExecutor for sync tool dispatch
tasks.rollback_notes: Single file revert — behavior identical, just less efficient.

tasks.title: Upgrade auto-memory with LLM-based classifier
tasks.rationale: Current heuristics are English-only keyword scoring. Korean (the primary UI language) is misclassified. Use one cheap LLM call for category+slug+should_save. Also fix silent exception swallowing.

subtasks.detail: New module. Class MemoryClassifier takes a Provider. Method classify(user_input, response) → dict {should_save: bool, category: 'entities'|'concepts'|'sessions', slug: str, title: str, tags: list[str]}. Prompt is bilingual (KO+EN) with few-shot examples. Output JSON strict. Timeout 5s. Fallback to heuristic on error or JSON parse failure.

In _auto_save_memory: if classifier available, call classify(); use its fields. If should_save=False, skip. Fallback to existing _is_memorable + _pick_category + _make_slug on any exception. Replace `except Exception: pass` with logger.warning(..., exc_info=True). Add env var BIRKIN_SMART_MEMORY=off to disable classifier.

Test with FakeProvider returning canned JSON. Cases: (1) valid classification → saved; (2) should_save=false → skipped; (3) invalid JSON → falls back to heuristic; (4) Korean input classified non-'sessions' when appropriate.

verification: Manual: have 5 Korean + 5 English conversations, verify memory/wiki/ has sensible categorization
acceptance_criteria: Korean input no longer defaults to 'sessions' when concept-like
Silent exception swallow replaced by logger.warning
BIRKIN_SMART_MEMORY=off disables LLM call path
Fallback path still works
tasks.commit_message: feat(memory): add LLM-based classifier for auto-save categorization
tasks.rollback_notes: Set BIRKIN_SMART_MEMORY=off or revert to restore heuristic-only path.

tasks.title: Make /api/chat async and non-blocking
tasks.rationale: Sync def chat() blocks the event loop when provider HTTP calls are slow. agent.achat and provider.acomplete are already implemented.

subtasks.detail: Change `def chat(body: ChatRequest)` → `async def chat(body: ChatRequest)`. Replace `agent.chat(body.message)` with `await agent.achat(body.message)`. Keep all error handling blocks intact.
If any chat test uses sync TestClient patterns, verify they still work (FastAPI TestClient handles async endpoints transparently).

verification: Load test: 10 concurrent POST /api/chat, verify no thread pool exhaustion
acceptance_criteria: chat endpoint is declared async
uses agent.achat
tests still pass
tasks.commit_message: perf(chat): convert /api/chat to async for non-blocking I/O
tasks.rollback_notes: Revert to sync def chat + agent.chat.

tasks.title: Replace context compression with summarization
tasks.rationale: Current 'keep head+tail' loses all middle context. Summarize via cheap model to preserve decisions and facts.

subtasks.detail: New module. Function summarize_messages(messages: list[Message], provider: Provider, model: Optional[str] = None) → str. Uses the provider's lightweight model (for anthropic, haiku-4-5). Prompt: 'Summarize the following conversation in 3-5 sentences, preserving: user goals, decisions made, key facts, unresolved questions.'  Return plain text. Cache by hash(tuple(msg.content for msg in messages)) to avoid re-summarizing same block.

Rewrite _compress_messages: count tokens (approximate via len(content)//4). If total > BIRKIN_CONTEXT_BUDGET_TOKENS (default 60000), split into head(KEEP_HEAD=2), mid (messages to compress), tail(KEEP_TAIL=16). Call summarize_messages(mid, self._provider). Insert summary as Message(role='system', content='[Conversation summary of earlier turns]\n{summary}'). Fallback to old slice-only behavior on summarization error.

Test with FakeProvider that records compression calls. Cases: (1) short history → no compression; (2) long history → summary call issued; (3) summarize fails → falls back to head+tail. Verify summary message is inserted between head and tail.

verification: Manual: 50-turn conversation, ask about fact from turn 5, verify still answered
acceptance_criteria: Long conversations trigger summarization not deletion
Summary is cached to prevent re-work
Fallback to head+tail slice on error
Configurable via env var
tasks.commit_message: feat(agent): replace truncation with LLM summarization for long context
tasks.rollback_notes: Revert compression.py import and restore static _compress_messages.

final_checks: Update ROADMAP.md — check off completed items
Update README — mention BIRKIN_AUTH_TOKEN, security model section
post_completion.release_proposal: Tag v0.2.0 after T10. Write release notes covering: new auth layer (breaking for exposed deployments), Anthropic model ID migration (breaking), smart context + memory.
